tasks.title: Let's get the Open Claw product running.
tasks.description: Deploy OpenClaw on a Linux VM, connect to Discord, and verify the AI responds to user messages.
tasks.plan:
## Overview

Deploy OpenClaw (personal AI assistant) on a Linux VM, configure Discord bot integration, and verify end-to-end messaging works.

## Phases

### Phase 1: Infrastructure & Prerequisites
- Provision Linux VM with Node 24+, npm/pnpm/bun
- Clone openclaw repo, install dependencies, create .env

### Phase 2: Discord Bot Configuration
- Register Discord bot application, obtain token
- Configure OpenClaw with Discord token and channel ID

### Phase 3: AI Model Integration
- Obtain LLM provider API key (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.)
- Add credentials to .env

### Phase 4: Launch and Verify
- Start OpenClaw service
- Test: user messages in Discord → AI responds within 5 seconds

## Success Criteria
- Linux VM has Node 24+ and openclaw installed
- Discord bot registered and configured
- OpenClaw service starts without errors
- User can send messages in Discord and receive AI responses

objective.statement: Get OpenClaw running on a Linux VM, connected to Discord, such that users can send messages and receive AI responses.
successCriteria: Linux VM provisioned with Node 24+ (or 22.16+), npm/pnpm/bun, git, and make
OpenClaw repository cloned and dependencies installed
Discord bot registered, token obtained, and configured in .env with DISCORD_TOKEN and channel settings
LLM provider API key obtained and configured in .env
OpenClaw service starts successfully (via 'openclaw onboard' or 'npm start')
End-to-end test passes: user sends message in Discord, OpenClaw responds within 5 seconds with coherent answer
nonGoals: Setting up multiple Discord servers or channels

proofUnits.title: Linux VM ready with Node and dependencies
proofUnits.expectedBehavior: Node.js 24+ (or 22.16+) is installed and accessible; npm/pnpm/bun is available; system has required build tools.
proofUnits.expectedPassingEvidence: All three commands print version strings. Node is ≥ v22.16.0 or v24.x.x.

proofUnits.title: OpenClaw cloned, dependencies installed, .env present
proofUnits.expectedBehavior: Repository cloned; npm install completes without errors; .env exists with DISCORD_TOKEN and provider fields.
proofUnits.expectedInitialFailure: .env file does not exist or node_modules/ missing.
proofUnits.expectedPassingEvidence: .env is readable; npm can list test files; no module not found errors.

proofUnits.title: Discord bot registered and token obtained
proofUnits.expectedBehavior: Discord bot created; token is valid alphanumeric string; bot invited to test server with permissions.
proofUnits.exactCommand:
1. Discord Developer Portal: Create app 'OpenClaw'
2. Add Bot
3. Copy token to .env as DISCORD_TOKEN=
4. Set Scopes: bot, Permissions: Send/Read Messages
5. Invite to test server
6. Verify bot in member list
proofUnits.expectedInitialFailure: Discord bot does not exist; no token.
proofUnits.expectedPassingEvidence: Bot appears in server; token in .env (100+ chars).
proofUnits.waiverGuidance: Manual—Discord token generation not CLI testable.

proofUnits.title: OpenClaw service starts and connects to Discord
proofUnits.expectedBehavior: npm start/openclaw onboard completes without error; logs show Discord connection success.
proofUnits.expectedPassingEvidence: Logs include 'connected' or 'listening'; exit 0 or timeout after 10s.
proofUnits.waiverGuidance: If openclaw onboard is interactive, run manually and confirm no errors.

proofUnits.title: End-to-end: Discord message → AI response
proofUnits.expectedBehavior: User sends message in Discord; within 5 seconds, OpenClaw responds with coherent answer.
proofUnits.exactCommand:
1. Service running (from unit 4)
2. Discord: send 'Hello, what is 2+2?'
3. Wait 5s for response
4. Send 'Tell me a joke'
5. Verify both responded coherently
proofUnits.expectedInitialFailure: Bot silent; no response after 5s; service crashed.
proofUnits.expectedPassingEvidence: Bot responds to both messages within 5s with coherent, relevant answers.
proofUnits.waiverGuidance: Manual—Discord interaction cannot be fully automated.
